decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective immediate action is to increase the heat spreader conductivity, which is currently at its upper bound of 500.0 W/(m*K). Since we cannot increase it further, we will instead increase the spreader width to enhance lateral heat spreading towards the cooled boundaries.

changes.reason: Increasing the heat spreader width maximizes the lateral area available for heat conduction towards the left and right Dirichlet boundaries (25°C), reducing thermal resistance and lowering the chip peak temperature.

expected_effects: Reduced thermal resistance in the lateral direction
Lower chip peak temperature moving closer to or below 85.0°C
More uniform temperature distribution across the spreader
risk_notes: The spreader width is approaching the design domain limit (1.2m); further geometric expansion may be limited.
If the temperature constraint is still violated, increasing base material conductivity may be required as a secondary measure.
